observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Schedule of Unreleased Checks to the Accounting Office before the year-end closing of the books; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks to present the accounts fairly in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the LDRRMF transactions leading to the incomplete transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and to use the prescribed format in Annex D in the presentation of LDRRMF; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the remaining unexpended balance of QRF to trust fund books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Undertake full opportunity on the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at the station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU; and
Create an Inventory Committee and require them to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan to be approved by the Local Chief Executive, and submit it to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Department Heads to initiate the return of any unserviceable properties under their jurisdiction of their offices to the Acting GSO; and
Instruct the Acting GSO to cancel the MR/ARE/PAR related to the unserviceable properties turned over, if identifiable, and initiate the disposal to avoid further deterioration due to natural elements and loss of value, generate additional funds if found valuable and sold.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Acting General Supply Officer to notify or to furnish the COA Audit Team with the copies of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports and the Delivery Receipts within twenty-four hours from acceptance; and
Require the Property Officer and Inspector to properly and completely accomplish the Acceptance and Inspection Report.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive appoint a permanent GSO in accordance with the qualification in RA 7160 and the Civil Service laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to release the shares from RPT and the financial aid to the barangays immediately; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to release the RPT shares and financial aid within its prescribed period.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Budget Officer grant a uniform rate of PERA to all its officials and employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to stop the payment of honorarium for observers and undertake necessary actions for the refund; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that claims for BAC honorarium are completely supported with an office order, attendance sheet, and certification that the procurement involves competitive bidding.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Secretariat and Municipal Engineer to prioritize and submit all lacking supporting documents of the subject contracts and to ensure that prospective contracts are submitted within five (5) days upon their perfection.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to explain the discrepancies noted to avoid the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance and ensure strict compliance with the DPWH DO No. 197 in preparing the projects' ABC.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to deduct the ten percent (10%) retention money from each progress payment of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all Department Heads, BAC Members, BAC Secretariat, and GSO to be meticulous and judicious in preparing their PPMP and Annual Procurement Plan so that procurements made by the municipality are properly planned and executed, and procurements made through alternative methods do not result in splitting government contracts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive limit the municipal officials who are allowed to reimburse expenses if it cannot be avoided, and consider establishing a revolving fund to meet the municipality's needs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting General Supply Office and Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that goods are described according to their type/kind and other relevant specifications in all transaction documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal HRM Officer to submit all Job Order Contracts; and
Require the JOs to submit the required DTR and accomplishment report as attachments to the payrolls, duly verified by the official who directly supervises the JO.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the General Service Officer to prepare and submit a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and
Direct all Municipal Drivers to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Official Travels.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately for audit inspection and verification all previously cancelled checks, and thereafter attach all cancelled checks to the Report of Checks Issued, to be forwarded to the Accounting Office for recording in the agency’s books of accounts, and for its eventual submission to the Audit Team for timely validation of the status of these checks.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the GF for the 20% DF, with complete SLs using the Sub-codes for this Special Account; prepare a post-closing trial Balance and Financial Statements for this Special Account and submit them to the Audit Team for verification in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO), Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO), and Municipal Accountant to stop charging insurance expenses and furniture and fixture expenses to the LDRRMF; and
Direct the MDRRMO to exclude administrative expenses from the LDRRMFIP as these do not directly contribute to the attainment of the key objectives of disaster risk reduction and management of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Local School Board to furnish copies of the SEF budget and supplemental budgets, if any, to the Municipal Budget Office, Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office within the prescribed period; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 on SEF utilization to the DepEd, copy furnished to the local Sangguniang concerned, Dep Ed and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that SEF disbursements are approved by proper officials; and
Require the BAC Chairman to cease the signing or approval of Purchase Orders.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use traveling expenses and food supplies expenses accounts correctly and strictly observe the description of accounts provided in NGAS in recording future financial transactions for proper presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to stop using current appropriation /allotment for payment of the prior year’s obligations/ transactions;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts all valid and legal expenditures at the end of the calendar year and shall be properly obligated as accounts payable in accordance with laws and regulations to preclude distortion of the current annual budget and possible misstatement of financial performance.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive the following:

In view of the computerization of the Commission On Audit in monitoring audit suspensions, disallowances and charges, to assign specific personnel to do the compliance of supporting documents and monitoring of it. Require the LDRRMC to establish properly the 5% calamity fund with proper allocation on the 70% preparedness fund and the 30% Quick Response Fund or stand-by fund for relief and recovery projects and activities.
Require the LDRRM Officer to:
Prepare the Investment Plan to support the unexpended balances of the compounded years as required by law.
Prepare monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRM Fund, certified correct by the Accountant, and submit to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
The Budget Officer to prepare separate registry of appropriations, allotments and obligations of the following:
RAAO for QRF
RAAO for MOOE
RAAO for CO
Require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the monthly 5% calamity fund not only based on IRA but to include other local collections.  Likewise, see to it that disbursements should be in accordance with the budget and the guidelines.
Further require both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
transfer only the 30% of the 5% calamity Fund and the unexpended balance of the 70% to be transferred to the trust fund account.
Refrain from disbursing in the trust fund account without actual declaration of calamity.

We recommended the following:
The GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres.
The Department Heads to identify the GAD related expenditures in their existing LGU Budget and Plan to be incorporated in the GAD Plan.
The GAD Focal Person should submit the amended GAD Plan and Budget and accomplishment report.

We recommended to:
Local Chief Executive to give emphasis on the collection of the delinquent real property taxes by applying the administrative remedies against delinquent taxpayers as provided for under the Local Government Code.
The Municipal Assessor to immediately submit the required documents.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial reports within the prescribed period in order to prepare and submit monthly trial balances on time for proper monitoring and timely decision making of the management and in order to immediately detect the errors made.
We strictly recommended to submit the monthly trial balances because the management had only submitted the December trial balances.

We recommended the following:
If budget warrants, the budget officer should appropriate funds for the creation of the internal audit department.
The Municipality should post the vacancy of the accountant in order to fill up the vacancy immediately.
Install internal control measures in safeguarding the assets of the municipality.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the human resource officer to furnish the Accounting office with a copy of the schedule of earned leave credits of LGU personnel with money value as basis in accounting for the leave accruals.

We recommended to the procuring department to submit the purchase order upon perfection of the contract and the Municipal General Service Officer to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24hours after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and / or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items so that timely inspection can be made by the COA.

We recommended to the Budget Officer to submit projects/ programs in line with the specific guidelines provided by DILG-DOE Circular No. 98-01 at the beginning of the year to serve as guide and reference in the release of funds charged to shares from national wealth. And should ensure that projects and programs to be implemented should be in accordance with the prescribed guidelines to avoid misuse and misapplication of the subject funds.

We have recommended the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive should create an appraisal / inventory committee to conduct an actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment headed by the Acting Property Officer to be witness by any representative of the Commission On Audit.
b. If no records could be found, start recording the actual existence of equipment, furniture and fixtures by office and if possible reconstruct the records.
c. Instruct the Acting Property Officer to provide property number of all properties, at the same time, maintained stock cards with the Municipal Accountant who will maintain a subsidiary ledger card.
d. Segregate the unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal.
e. The Municipal Accountant should prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
f. The Municipal Administrator should update the list of properties that need to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS.
g. Provide budget for the insurance premium of properties identified to be insured especially building to protect the interest of the government.
h. Acting Property Officer to submit the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to the accounting division; maintain SLs for the inventory accounts; and to strictly comply with the provision of Section 12 of MNGAS, Volume II and Section 114 of PD 1445.
i. Municipal Budget Officer to include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation of fidelity bond in order that the property officer can apply for a bond.

We have recommended the following:
a. The Municipal Mayor should arrange for the new mode of delivery of the bank statements either by pick-up by an authorized personnel or by registered mail considering the poor services made by the Post Office of the Municipality.
b. If not possible, the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer as signatories of the bank accounts should authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll in online access of their accounts so that bank statements could be readily available at any time.
c. The Municipal Accountant to find ways to secure the bank statements so that bank reconciliation statements can be prepared making their cash in bank balance as accurate as possible.
d. Request the Local Chief Executive to augment the work force of the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording and preparation of the reports.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to give emphasis on the collection of the delinquent real property taxes by applying the administrative remedies against delinquent taxpayers as provided for under the Local Government Code.

We have recommended the following:
a. The concerned accountable offices should submit monthly the required reports and not to accumulate them on a lump sum basis so that from time to time, the audit team can update on the financial status of the Municipality.
b. We also require the national offices such as the DA, DILG, DSWD, DOLE and other offices to secure liquidation reports before transferring additional funds to the said LGU in order not to delay the implementation of their programs and projects and to avoid the possible misappropriation or losses or projects not implemented especially during transition period.
We have recommended to the BAC / BAC Secretariat to submit the contracts together with the documentary requirements enumerated above with the checklist for submission to the COA Technical.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2015 and 2016 and that management to strictly comply with the submission of AAPSI in the ensuing annual reports.

We have recommended to the Budget Officer to submit projects/ programs in line with the specific guidelines provided by DILG-DOE Circular No. 98-01 at the beginning of the year to serve as guide and reference in the release of funds charged to shares from national wealth. And should ensure that projects and programs to be implemented should be in accordance with the prescribed guidelines to avoid misuse and misapplication of the subject funds.

We have recommended the following:
a. The Municipal Mayor to recommend her to attend training that involved budgeting and preparation of the registries.
b. The Municipal Budget Officer should maintain the required registries of funds / appropriations / allotments and obligations and utilization in order to avoid juggling of funds and for internal control purposes. Also, to facilitate the realization of the programs / projects / activities embodied in the Approved Annual Budget.
c. Strict adherence to the provision of laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended the following:
a. The Municipal Mayor to create a GAD committee 
b. We also have recommended to the GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres.  
c. Further, incorporate the approved GAD plan/budget in the budget proposal for CY 2018 and onwards to ensure that the cost of implementing the GAD activities is part of the approved budget and that at least 5% of the total agency appropriations as authorized under the annual GAA, shall correspond to the activities supporting the GAD.
d. The Department Heads to identify the GAD related expenditures in their existing LGU Budget and Plan to be incorporated in the GAD Plan.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal DRRM Officer to prepare monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRM Fund, certified correct by the Accountant, and submit to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We have recommended the following:
a. The Local School Board to prepare a budget for Special Education Fund and make deliberations in accordance with the guidelines.
b. The proceeds shall be allocated for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development as determined and approved by the local school board.

We have recommended the following:

observations.managementAction:
During the interviews, the Acting Property Officer averred that she did attempt to conduct physical inventory however stopped due that she finds difficulty locating prior year records. Local officials concerned admitted these deficiencies and only recognized the necessity of an appraisal/inventory committee to conduct physical inventory. But this was not able to complete due to the voluminous workload of the budget officer. The Municipal Budget Officer was the Acting Property Officer in addition to her duties and obligations as Budget Officer and that no funding was allotted for the property bond.

Due to manpower constraint, the Municipal Accountant was not able to maintain subsidiary ledgers. Therefore, property and inventory accountability cannot be established.

The Municipal Accountant explained that supplies expenses were directly recorded as expenses and given directly to the procuring department considering that the management did not purchase by bulk but only as need arises. She also admitted that no subsidiary ledgers for the property accounts.

In the reimbursement of official calls, claimants / department heads shall attach the call logs showing the names and remarks as official or personal calls as well as the statement of accounts.

We have recommended the following:
a. 	The Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the budget in compliance with the limitations set by law.
b.	Submit explanations for the charging of expenses.
c.	Refrain from the reimbursement of expenses not mentioned above as allowed under extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses.
We have recommended to the BAC / BAC Secretariat to submit the contracts together with the documentary requirements enumerated above with the checklist for submission to the COA Technical.

We have recommended the following:
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant are required to strictly comply with the regulations relative to the submission of monthly reports and supporting documents to the Auditor within ten (10) days from receipt of said documents pursuant to 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
We invite the attention of the DILG, DSWD, DA and other government agencies that provided fund transfer to the Municipality to secure liquidation reports with the attached approved disbursement vouchers duly audited by the assigned auditor in the Municipality to avoid unliquidated fund transfer which exposes the funds of the government to misappropriation and losses.

We have recommended the following:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit a timely Annual Budget for the LGU in order that all disbursements were properly backed up with legal basis. Also, to prepare the SAAOB and submit to the Audit Team thru the Municipal Accountant.
Direct the Local Chief Executive to adhere strictly with the provisions of Republic Act No. 7160 in the preparation and submission of the Annual Budget as well as the Supplemental Budget if any.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal DRRM Officer to prepare monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRM Fund, certified correct by the Accountant, and submit to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We have recommended the following:
a.	The Local School Board to prepare a budget for Special Education Fund and make deliberations in accordance with the guidelines.
b.	The proceeds shall be allocated for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development as determined and approved by the local school board.

We have recommended the in-charge of the program, the MSWD to prepare an individual chart of the child in order to properly monitor the nutritional status of the child and likewise submit the accomplishment report.

We have recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor should apply for a fidelity bond, approved the disbursement vouchers for the appropriation of the sanggunian only and shall be allowed to countersigned in the check drawn.
observations.managementAction: #1 | The Municipal Vice Mayor were already bonded in CY 2018.

Strict adherence to the provision of laws, rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant to assign personnel from Accounting Office to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all Cash Collecting Officers to determine their monthly collections tracing the accountable forms issued before remitting it to the Liquidating Officer.
The Local Chief Executive should request that these Collecting Officers be sent to a seminar for proper orientation on their assigned work. Request the COA Audit Team Leader to orient on the preparation of subsidiary ledgers by Collecting Officers.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to require all revenue collectors to adhere strictly with the regulation to ensure effective performance on the task assigned and submit monthly report of collections and report of accountable forms and turnover the used accountable forms. And used and issued accountable forms in sequence.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer should closely monitor the deposit of collections to prevent possible loss or misappropriation of government funds handled by the revenue collecting officers.

We have recommended to the Collecting Officers to submit the Report of collections and deposits to the accounting division; Municipal Accountant to maintain SLs for the individual collectors; and to hire qualified employee to be assigned in the office of the municipal accountant.

We have recommended to the Municipal Accountant to get the monthly bank statements from the depository banks or enroll the bank accounts to online access in order to prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements monthly in adherence to Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-11.

We have recommended to the management to give emphasis on the collection of the delinquent real property taxes by applying the administrative remedies against delinquent taxpayers as provided for under the Local Government Code. We have further recommended to management to hire additional employee assigned in the office of the assessor, otherwise, require the concerned employee to render overtime in order to prepare and submit the estimated real property tax collectibles.
We have recommended to the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation of fidelity bond in order that the property officer can apply for a bond.

We have recommended to the Management to create an appraisal / inventory committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment.  Also instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers. After physical count and report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger card and should bear a Property Number. The municipal accountant is to provide residual value in computing the depreciation.  We have also recommended to the Administrative Officer to update the list of properties that needs to be insured and submit the same to GSIS. This will guarantee that all properties are adequately covered by insurance in order to protect the interest of the government. For lost / damaged properties without covering insurance, liability therefore should devolve upon the parties responsible.

We have recommended to the Supply Officer to submit the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to the accounting division; maintain SLs for the inventory accounts; and to strictly comply with the provision of Section 12 of MNGAS, Volume II and Section 114 of PD 1445.

We have recommended to the Local School Board to prepare and submit Annual SEF Budget. And the proceeds shall be allocated for the operation and maintenances of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development as determined and approved by the local school board.
We have recommended to the MSWD to submit the accomplishment report.

Strict adherence to the provision of laws, rules and regulations.
Attend to the seminar involving budgeting purposes. Request the COA Audit Team Leader to orient on the preparation of registries per NGAS.

We have recommended to the Human Resource Officer furnishing the Accounting office with a copy of Schedule of Earned Leave Credits for all LGU personnel with money value as basis by the Accounting Office to record the leave accruals. Meantime, that budget was not yet provided, just disclosed it in the Notes to Financial Statements.

The Municipal Accountant to assign personnel from Accounting Office to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all Cash Collecting Officers to determine their monthly collections tracing the accountable forms issued before remitting it to the Liquidating Officer.
The Local Chief Executive should request that these Collecting Officers be sent to a seminar for proper orientation on their assigned work. Request the COA Audit Team Leader to orient on the preparation of subsidiary ledgers by Collecting Officers.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to require all revenue collectors to adhere strictly with the regulation to ensure effective performance on the task assigned and submit monthly report of collections and report of accountable forms and turnover the used accountable forms. And used and issued accountable forms in sequence.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer should closely monitor the deposit of collections to prevent possible loss or misappropriation of government funds handled by the revenue collecting officers.

We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality.
She should prepare necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income and stale checks to bring the cash in bank account in its correct balance.
She should prepare a letter informing the servicing bank of the wrong postings they made if any and secure copies of debit memos to validate those amounts debited by the bank without any corresponding entries in the books.
The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer as signatories of the bank accounts of the Municipality should authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll online access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.

We have recommended the following:
The Committee on Local Finance of the Municipality should give emphasis on the collection of delinquent real property taxes by applying the administrative remedies against delinquent taxpayers as provided for under Section 345 of Republic Act 7160.
The Local Chief Executive should hire additional employees to augment the Office of the Municipal Assessor, otherwise, require the concerned employees to render overtime in order to prepare and submit the estimated real property tax collectibles of the Municipality.
And if possible avail of the Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment Collection System (E-TRACS) installed by the Provincial Government of Lanao del Norte.
We have recommended to the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation of fidelity bond in order that the property officer can apply for a bond.

We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive should create an appraisal / inventory committee to conduct an actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment headed by the General Services Officer to be witness by any representative of the Commission On Audit.
If no records could be found, start recording the actual existence of equipment, furniture and fixtures by office and if possible reconstruct the records.
Instruct the General Service Officer to provide property number of all properties, at the same time, maintained stock cards with the Municipal Accountant who will maintain a subsidiary ledger card.
Segregate the unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal.
The Municipal Accountant should prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
The Municipal Administrator should update the list of properties that need to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS.
Provide budget for the insurance premium of properties identified to be insured especially building to protect the interest of the government.

We have recommended that the Supply Officer to submit the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to the accounting division; maintain SLs for the inventory accounts; and to strictly comply with the provision of Section 12 of MNGAS, Volume II and Section 114 of PD 1445.

We have recommended the following:
The Local School Board to prepare a budget for Special Education Fund and make deliberations in accordance with the guidelines.
The proceeds shall be allocated for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development as determined and approved by the local school board.

We have recommended to the Municipal Mayor to issue designation order for the designation of a collecting officer and to allot in the annual budget additional fidelity bond for the municipal collectors and to post a fidelity bond to the Bureau of Treasury.
